summary:
Investigated ISS-1104 (SEC-2b.5 WIN-NNN namespace collision). Confirmed the ambiguity is real and has two concrete collisions: (i) our WIN-015 (Meyl scalar Faraday) ≠ dome's WIN-015 (SAA Decay Rate 77=77 nT/yr) — same label, different claims; (ii) the dome cites WIN-075 and WIN-076 which do not exist in our wins.json (last primary id is 069). Also confirmed our review uses the 'WIN-NNN' format canonically across every section (300+ occurrences in data/sections.json). EXP-177 proposes two surgical patches: P1 inserts a one-sentence 'Notation' paragraph at the top of the Domain-by-domain audit (between the categories
    and the

    Tesla-family

    subsection) explaining the labels are dome-side, naming the two concrete collisions; P2 tweaks the closing 'Critically, WIN-024 appears twice ...' sentence to prefix 'the dome's' four times and strengthens the double-count argument (boundary vs wind-speed). Both patches preserve the verbatim source-string quotations the dome publishes. Verified both current_text strings match sections.json part2b.html exactly. Recommended action: patch (apply both P1 and P2, close ISS-1104). TLDR unchanged.
